task_description: Search for a one-way flight from Los Angeles to San Francisco under a low price cap and select the basic economy fare to proceed with booking.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the top navigation bar of the United Airlines website. A semitransparent red X is centered on the rounded dark-blue "Booking" button with a small downward-pointing arrow, next to the United globe logo and before "Manage my trips." In the full-screen view, this is a website open in Chrome, with the main booking panel visible below showing tabs like Book, Flight status, Check-in, and My trips, plus flight search fields and promotional panels. The target is a top-area UI control in the site header.
caption.action: Click the "Booking" dropdown button in the top navigation bar.
caption.expectation: The Booking control should become active, likely opening a dropdown menu or switching focus to booking-related options.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a white dropdown panel section titled "Book a flight" with three blue underlined links. A semitransparent red X is centered on the "Shop by map" link, which is outlined with a dark focus rectangle. In the full-screen view, this dropdown is open from the United Airlines website header under the selected "Booking" menu, with adjacent columns such as "Add to your trip" and "Find travel credits" visible over the homepage flight search area. The target is a UI control in the dropdown menu.
caption.action: Click the "Shop by map" link in the open Booking dropdown menu.
caption.expectation: The site should open the Shop by map page or switch the current page to a map-based flight shopping view.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the route map search form with a rounded white input field labeled "Flying from." A semitransparent red X is centered inside that input, which currently contains the text "Hong Kong HKG." Above it, a red-outlined warning banner says "Sorry, no results have been found." In the full-screen view, this is the United Airlines "Explore destinations" interactive map page in Chrome, with nearby controls for Search type, Dates, Max price, a blue Search button, a collapsed "List of search results" panel, and a large North America map below. The target is a form input control in the page content area.
caption.action: Click the "Flying from" input field containing "Hong Kong HKG."
caption.expectation: The origin field should become active for editing or selection, allowing the departure airport text to be changed or confirmed.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop would center on the active white text field labeled "Flying from," outlined in blue, where the previous origin text has been replaced and the visible entry now reads "LA" as the user is typing a new airport code. In the full-screen view, this is the United Airlines "Explore destinations" page in Chrome, with a red warning banner stating no results were found, adjacent controls for Search type, Dates, Max price, a blue Search button, a collapsed "List of search results" panel, and a large map of the United States below.
caption.action: Type "LAX" into the active "Flying from" field, replacing the previous origin entry.
caption.expectation: The origin field should display "LAX," and the page may prepare matching airport suggestions or update the search context for Los Angeles.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows the autocomplete dropdown beneath the active "Flying from" field. A semitransparent red X is centered on the first suggestion, "Los Angeles, CA, US (LAX)," which is outlined in a dark focus rectangle. A second suggestion, "Burbank, CA, US (BUR)," is visible below. In the full-screen view, this is the United Airlines Explore destinations page in Chrome, with "LAX" typed in the origin field, a red warning banner about no results, controls for search type, dates, max price, a blue Search button, and a large map below. The target is a suggestion item in the dropdown list.
caption.action: Click the "Los Angeles, CA, US (LAX)" suggestion in the Flying from dropdown list.
caption.expectation: The suggestion list should close, and the origin field should be set to Los Angeles (LAX) as the selected departure airport.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the Search type dropdown expanded beneath a rounded white field. A semitransparent red X is centered on the currently displayed value "Roundtrip" inside the dropdown control, with a black downward chevron on the right. The open menu below lists two options: "Roundtrip" highlighted in blue and "One Way" beneath it. In the full-screen view, this is the United Airlines Explore destinations page in Chrome, with "Los Angeles LAX" in the Flying from field, a red warning banner about no results, a Dates field set to "Flexible dates," a Max price field, a blue Search button, and a large map below. The target is a UI control in the form area, specifically the search-type selector.
caption.action: Click the "Roundtrip" search type dropdown control.
caption.expectation: The search type menu should remain open or refocus on the selector, keeping the current selection active and ready for choosing a different trip type.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows the Search type dropdown expanded below the rounded selector. A semitransparent red X is centered on the menu item labeled "One Way," which appears as the second option beneath "Roundtrip." In the full-screen view, this is the United Airlines Explore destinations page in Chrome, with "Los Angeles LAX" in the Flying from field, the Dates field set to "Flexible dates," a Max price field, a blue Search button, a red warning banner, and a map of North America below. The target is a UI control in the open dropdown menu.
caption.action: Click the "One Way" option in the Search type dropdown menu.
caption.expectation: The dropdown menu should close, and the Search type field should update from "Roundtrip" to "One Way."

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the Dates field in the search form, outlined in blue to indicate focus, with the text "Flexible dates" and a small blue calendar icon on the right side. A semitransparent red X is centered over this Dates field, indicating the target is this UI control. In the full-screen view, this is the United Airlines Explore destinations page in Chrome, with "Los Angeles LAX" selected in Flying from, "One Way" selected in Search type, a Max price field to the right, a blue Search button, a red warning banner above, and a large map below.
caption.action: Click the Dates field labeled "Flexible dates" with the calendar icon.
caption.expectation: The date-selection interface should open or the Dates control should become active for choosing travel dates.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a white date-selection popover with two tabs near the top. A semitransparent red X is centered on the left tab labeled "Exact Dates," which has a dark outline, while the neighboring tab "Flexible Dates" is visible to the right with a blue underline. In the full-screen view, this popover is open above the map on the United Airlines Explore destinations page in Chrome. The search form shows "Los Angeles LAX" as the origin, "One Way" as the search type, a Max price field, and a blue Search button, with a red warning banner above and a map below. The target is a UI control inside the open date picker.
caption.action: Click the "Exact Dates" tab in the open date-selection popover.
caption.expectation: The date picker should switch from the flexible dates view to the exact dates view, showing controls for selecting specific travel dates.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows the April 2026 calendar in the open date picker. A semitransparent red X is centered on the blue highlighted day cell labeled "1" under the Wednesday column, indicating that exact date tile is the target. In the full-screen view, this calendar popover is open on the United Airlines Explore destinations page in Chrome, above the map and below the search form with "Los Angeles LAX," "One Way," a Max price field, and a blue Search button. The click is on a UI control inside the date-selection popover.
caption.action: Click the blue date tile labeled "1" in the April 2026 calendar.
caption.expectation: That date should become selected as the departure date, and the date picker should reflect the chosen exact date or advance to the next date-selection state.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the search form's "Max price" field with a blue focus outline. Inside the rounded white input box, the text "$2000" is visible on the left, small up/down spinner arrows are on the right, and a blue sliders/settings-style icon sits at the far right edge. A semitransparent red X is centered directly over the "$2000" value, so the target is this max-price input control. In the full-screen view, this is on the United Airlines Explore destinations page in Chrome, above a large map, alongside the fields "Flying from" set to "Los Angeles LAX," "Search type" set to "One Way," the date field showing "Apr 01," and a blue "Search" button. This is a UI control in the search form, not the map.
caption.action: Click the "Max price" input showing "$2000".
caption.expectation: The max-price field should become active for editing, placing focus in the input and allowing the value to be changed.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop centers on the active rounded white "Max price" input in the search form. The field has a blue focus outline and now shows "$000," indicating the previous "$2000" value has been replaced except for the currency symbol and trailing zeros, with the typed digit affecting the numeric content. Small spinner arrows and a blue sliders/settings-style icon remain on the right edge of the field. In the full-screen view, this field sits on the United Airlines Explore destinations page beside "Flying from" set to "Los Angeles LAX," "Search type" set to "One Way," the date field showing "Apr 01," and the blue "Search" button above a North America map.
caption.action: Type "1" into the active Max price field, replacing the selected existing numeric value rather than appending.
caption.expectation: The max-price entry should update to reflect the typed digit in the field, with focus remaining in the input for possible further editing.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image centers on a large rounded dark-blue button labeled "Search" in white text, with a semitransparent red X directly over the label, indicating the exact target is this button. Immediately to its left is a small sliders/settings icon button, and below is the map. In the full-screen view, this button is part of the search form on the United Airlines "Explore destinations" page, next to fields showing "Flying from" as "Los Angeles LAX," "Search type" as "One Way," the date "Apr 01," and max price "$1000." A pink outlined warning banner above the form reads, "Sorry, no results have been found. Please enter a different origin location or expand your search area." This is a UI control in the form area.
caption.action: Click the dark-blue "Search" button.
caption.expectation: The search should run with the current filters, causing the page to refresh or update the results/map, while the button briefly shows a pressed state.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows a semitransparent red X on a plain light-gray/white rectangular area directly beneath the search form and just above the map, at the left side of the map section. It is not on a button, field, pin, or other distinct control—this is a blank region of the page. In the full-screen view, the United Airlines Explore destinations page is visible with the search controls at the top, a white "List of search results" panel above the map on the left, and a large destination map below; the X lands in the empty area near the top-left edge of the map section rather than on any labeled UI element.
caption.action: Click the blank region above the map near the left side of the map section.
caption.expectation: Nothing significant should open or change; at most, any current input focus may be cleared while the page remains in the same state.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows the upper portion of the United Airlines results area with no red X, indicating a scroll action rather than a click. Visible are the "List of search results" panel header on the left, the search form above with fields for origin, search type, date, and max price, the dark-blue "Search" button, and the destination map on the right filled with many blue dots and white fare labels. In the full-screen view, this is the United "Explore destinations" page in Chrome, with the page already positioned near the top of the results section and a cookie banner still spanning the bottom of the window.
caption.action: Scroll up on the page to move the content upward toward earlier content above the current results section.
caption.expectation: The page should shift upward slightly, revealing more content above the current map-and-results area or moving closer to the top of the page.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the rounded white fare pill in the first search result, specifically over the right side of the pill that reads "Wed, Apr 1 from $188" next to "San Francisco, CA, US (SFO)." This is a clickable result control inside the results list, not a blank area. In the full-screen view, the United Airlines "Explore destinations" page is open in Chrome with the search form at the top, a left panel titled "List of search results," and a large destination map on the right. The targeted result is the top item in the list.
caption.action: Click the rounded fare pill labeled "Wed, Apr 1 from $188" for "San Francisco, CA, US (SFO)."
caption.expectation: The San Francisco result should become selected, likely highlighting the item and updating the map or result details to focus on that destination.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows the full Chrome window with no semitransparent red X, indicating a scroll action affecting the page rather than a specific control. Visible on the United Airlines booking page are the route fields set from LAX to SFO, the date set to Apr 01, the travelers field showing 1 Adult, a price display dropdown set to Money, an Update button, a large promotional credit-card banner, filter chips such as Sort, Stops, Fare Type, Duration, Connections, Refundable fares, Aircraft, and Wheelchair, and the fare results section headed "DEPART ON: April 1" with cabin columns including United Economy showing $188. A cookie consent banner spans the bottom of the page. The full-screen view matches this same page state, showing the flight results list area below the filters.
caption.action: Scroll down on the page to move further into the flight results below the currently visible fare cards.
caption.expectation: The page should shift downward, revealing more of the available flight options and details lower in the results list.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X placed directly over the text link labeled "Select flight" with a right-pointing arrow inside a light blue fare card. This is a clickable control within the flight results card, not a blank area. In the full-screen view, Chrome is open on the United Airlines flight results page for LAX to SFO on April 1. The results section shows fare columns such as United Economy, Economy Plus, and United First, and the targeted "Select flight" link is in the first light blue United Economy card priced at $188 next to the top nonstop flight option.
caption.action: Click the "Select flight" link with the right-pointing arrow in the light blue $188 United Economy fare card.
caption.expectation: The selected fare option should open or advance to the next booking step, likely showing flight details or fare selection for that itinerary.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the full Chrome window with no semitransparent red X, indicating a scroll action on the page rather than a specific control. The visible United Airlines booking page shows a selected nonstop flight from LAX to SFO with times 7:15 AM to 8:49 AM, a blue United Economy fare tile showing From $188 with a "Select fare" label, and below it a large section titled "Select a fare to continue" containing three fare cards: Basic (Most restrictive) priced at $188, Standard priced at $223, and Flexible (Fully refundable) priced at $263. A cookie consent banner stretches across the bottom, and a vertical "Site feedback" tab appears on the right edge. The full-screen view matches this fare-selection page state.
caption.action: Scroll down on the page to view more of the fare selection details and options below the currently visible cards.
caption.expectation: The page should move downward, revealing additional content lower in the fare selection section, such as more fare details or continuation controls.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on a small empty square checkbox outlined in dark blue near the bottom of the left fare card, immediately beside the text starting with "Basic Economy works for me." This is a selectable UI control, not a blank area. In the full-screen view, Chrome displays the United Airlines fare selection page with three cards under "Select a fare to continue": Basic (Most restrictive) on the left, Standard in the middle, and Flexible (Fully refundable) on the right. The targeted checkbox is inside the left Basic fare card above a disabled gray "Select" button.
caption.action: Click the checkbox next to "Basic Economy works for me." in the left Basic fare card.
caption.expectation: The checkbox should become selected, indicating acknowledgment of the Basic Economy restrictions, and the gray "Select" button for that fare should become available or the card should update to allow continuing.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the blue rounded rectangular "Select" button with a black outline inside the left Basic fare card. Above it, the checkbox next to "Basic Economy works for me." is already checked, so this is the active selection control. In the full-screen view, Chrome is on the United Airlines fare selection page under "Select a fare to continue," showing three fare cards: Basic on the left, Standard in the middle, and Flexible on the right. The targeted button is in the left Basic (Most restrictive) card priced at $188.
caption.action: Click the blue "Select" button in the left Basic fare card.
caption.expectation: The Basic fare should be chosen and the booking flow should advance to the next step, such as traveler details, trip review, or additional booking options.
